providers.title: Care Providers
providers.subtitle: Manage local care providers, their services, and verification status.
providers.confirm_deactivate: Deactivate this provider? It will no longer appear in member-facing lists.
providers.confirm_delete: Delete this provider permanently? This cannot be undone.

about.title: About care providers
about.body: Care providers are local organisations or individuals offering services to community members.
about.types_body: Providers can be home care, transport, meal delivery, companionship, or other categories.
about.verification_body: Verified providers have been reviewed by an administrator and have passed identity and credential checks.

placeholders.description: A short description of the provider and their services…
placeholders.name: e.g. Acme Home Care

hour_transfers.title: Hour Transfers
hour_transfers.subtitle: Review and process incoming and pending hour transfers between members.

reject_modal.reason_placeholder: Explain why this transfer is being rejected…
reject_modal.title: Reject transfer

loyalty.title: Loyalty Program
loyalty.description: Configure how members redeem time credits for discounts at participating merchants.

about.title: About the loyalty program
about.body: Members can redeem time credits at participating merchants in exchange for a discount on goods or services.
about.rate_body: Defines how much currency one time credit hour is worth at the merchant point of sale.
about.discount_body: The maximum percentage of a purchase that can be paid for with time credits.

errors.rate_positive: Exchange rate must be a positive number.

reverse_modal.body: Reversing a redemption will refund the time credits to the member.
reverse_modal.reason_placeholder: Why is this redemption being reversed?
reverse_modal.title: Reverse redemption

settings.accept_time_credits_hint: Allow this merchant to accept time credits as payment for a portion of each purchase.
settings.description: Configure the merchant-side rate and maximum-discount policy for the loyalty program.
settings.exchange_rate_description: How much currency a single time credit hour is worth at the merchant point of sale.
settings.maximum_discount_description: The maximum percentage of a purchase that can be paid using time credits.
settings.title: Loyalty settings
settings.view_only: You have read-only access to this setting.

feedback.title: Municipality Feedback
feedback.subtitle: Review feedback submitted by community members.

trust_tier.title: Trust Tier Configuration
trust_tier.subtitle: Set the criteria thresholds for each trust level. Changes apply on the next recompute run.
trust_tier.recompute_hint: Use recompute when thresholds change and existing members need to be re-evaluated immediately. Runs in the background and may take 30-60 seconds.

meta.title: Trust Tier Configuration
meta.description: Configure trust tier thresholds, recompute member tiers, and review what each tier unlocks.

about.title: About this page
about.body: Trust tiers are a reputation ladder that unlocks privileges as members become more active and verified. Members advance automatically when they meet the criteria thresholds below. Use recompute to force an immediate recalculation for all members. Tier progression affects the Warmth Pass, coordinator eligibility, and visibility in the member directory.

member.label: Member
member.description: Basic community member with at least 1 hour logged.

trusted.label: Trusted
trusted.description: Active member with several hours and reviews.

coordinator.label: Coordinator
coordinator.description: Highly active verified member eligible to coordinate.

1.name: Member
1.unlocks: Can post help listings and receive care hours.

2.name: Trusted
2.unlocks: Eligible for Warmth Pass and can be recommended for care.

3.name: Verified
3.unlocks: Full care coordinator visibility and identity badge.

4.name: Coordinator
4.unlocks: Eligible to approve hours and coordinate matches.

errors.load_failed: Failed to load trust tier configuration.
errors.save_failed: Failed to save trust tier settings.

messages.saved: Trust tier configuration saved. Changes apply on the next recompute run.

trust_tier.recompute_confirm: Recompute trust tiers for all members? This may take a few minutes.

operating_policy.title: Operating Policy
operating_policy.subtitle: Caring Community operating-policy workshop: rules software cannot guess.

meta.title: Operating Policy
meta.description: Configure approval, safeguarding, SLA, and social-value policy rules for the care programme.

about.title: About this page
about.body: Operating Policy defines the rules of your community care programme: who can approve hours, how quickly coordinators must respond, how social value is calculated, and what the reporting cycle looks like. Configure this before launching the pilot. Changes take effect immediately and are logged for audit purposes.
about.methodology_middle: hourly rate and prevention multiplier fields use the
about.methodology_suffix: hourly rate and prevention multiplier fields use the Caring Community methodology. If your community uses a different currency or local care economics, set the rate to your local formal-care hourly equivalent and the multiplier to 1.0.
about.methodology_tail: pilot, set the rate to your local formal-care hourly equivalent and the multiplier to 1.0.

approval_authority.label: Approval authority
approval_authority.help: Who may finalise approved hours: tenant admin, coordinators, or both parties by mutual approval.

trusted_reviewer_threshold.label: Trusted-reviewer threshold (hours logged)
trusted_reviewer_threshold.help: Minimum approved hours before a member becomes a trusted reviewer or reaches Trusted tier.

sla_first_response_hours.label: SLA: first response (hours)
sla_first_response_hours.help: Target time to first response on a new help request.

sla_help_request_hours.label: SLA: help-request resolution (hours)
sla_help_request_hours.help: Target time to close or match a help request.

legacy_hour_settlement.label: Legacy-hour settlement policy
legacy_hour_settlement.help: How a deceased member's remaining hours are settled.

reciprocal_balance_threshold_hours.label: Reciprocal-balance intervention threshold (hours)
reciprocal_balance_threshold_hours.help: When a member's give/receive imbalance triggers a coordinator check-in.

safeguarding_escalation_user_id.label: Safeguarding escalation owner (user ID)
safeguarding_escalation_user_id.help: User ID receiving safeguarding escalations. Leave blank to use admins as a group.

chf_hourly_rate.label: CHF social value per hour
chf_hourly_rate.help: CHF value per care hour for ROI reporting using the Swiss formal-care assistant rate.

chf_prevention_multiplier.label: CHF prevention multiplier
chf_prevention_multiplier.help: Multiplier applied to formal-care offset for the Caring Community and Caring Community methodology.

statement_cadence.label: Member statement cadence
statement_cadence.help: How often members receive a per-member hour statement.

policy_appendix_url.label: Signed policy appendix URL
policy_appendix_url.help: Paste the public URL of the signed policy agreement between your cooperative and the municipality. This appears in audit reports and the municipal reporting pack.

messages.saved: Operating policy saved. Changes apply to all new exchanges immediately.

warmth_pass.title: Warmth Pass
warmth_pass.subtitle: Look up a member to check Warmth Pass eligibility and recognised contribution history.

about.title: About Warmth Pass
about.body: Warmth Pass recognises members who have made sustained, verified contributions to their community.

errors.no_data: No data available for this member.

not_eligible_notice.title: Not eligible yet
not_eligible_notice.body: This member does not yet meet the requirements for a Warmth Pass.

meta.title: Caring Community
meta.description: Configure the integrated care hub, dependent capabilities, and municipal reporting surfaces.

about.title: About this page
about.body: The Caring Community module enables your timebank to coordinate informal neighbourhood care, matching residents who need help with trusted members who can provide it. It adds a trust-tier system, coordinator workflow, pilot evaluation tools, and municipal reporting on top of your existing timebank.

getting_started.title: Getting started
getting_started.step_1: Configure Operating Policy: set your approval workflow and CHF hourly rate.
getting_started.step_2: Capture a pre-pilot baseline in the Pilot Scoreboard.
getting_started.step_4: Run the Pilot Launch Readiness check.
getting_started.step_5: Once launched, use the Coordinator Workflow as your daily operations dashboard.

switch.title: Module kill switch
switch.description: When this switch is off, the Caring Community route, navigation entry, dashboard cards, and quick-create actions are hidden.
switch.dependency_note: Caring Community relies on the modules and features listed below being enabled. A disabled module will limit coordinator and reporting features.

reporting.title: Municipal reporting
reporting.description: Jump into the reporting surfaces needed for canton, municipality, and cooperative conversations.
reporting.note: These surfaces use existing NEXUS reporting today and are ready for Caring Community exports next.

errors.load_failed: Failed to load Caring Community configuration

intro.title: About this page - use with caution
intro.body: Emergency Alerts are urgent broadcasts sent to all members (or a targeted sub-group) immediately. Use this only for genuine emergencies - severe weather, community safety incidents, or urgent care coverage gaps. Alerts are sent as push notifications and in-app banners. They cannot be unsent once dispatched.
intro.targeting_body: Selecting severity "Danger" or "Warning" sends to all members. Draft carefully - there is no recall once broadcast.
intro.delivery_body: Alerts are delivered within 60 seconds of dispatch.

form.title: Title
form.body_placeholder: Describe the emergency and any actions members should take...
form.expires_at_description: Leave blank for no automatic expiry
form.confirm_suffix: and display a persistent banner on all pages. I confirm this is a genuine emergency.

meta.title: Caring Community Workflow
meta.description: This is your daily operations dashboard. Use it to review pending hour approvals, monitor overdue items, manage coordinator assignments, generate member statements, and configure workflow policy. Pending items that have exceeded their SLA appear highlighted in amber.

toast.load_failed: Could not load caring community workflow.

pending.title: Pending Reviews
pending.description_prefix: Hours awaiting coordinator review and approval. Items marked
pending.description_middle: have exceeded the review SLA; items marked
pending.description_suffix: have exceeded the escalation SLA. Approve or decline each submission, or assign it to a specific coordinator.
pending.empty: Nothing pending. The review queue is clear.

paper_onboarding.title: Paper onboarding (OCR)
paper_onboarding.description: Upload a scanned form or photo. We'll extract the fields and let you review before creating the account.
paper_onboarding.file_required: Choose a file to upload first.
paper_onboarding.load_failed: Could not load paper onboarding intakes.

tandem.title: Tandem Suggestions
tandem.description: AI-suggested supporter-recipient pairs based on location, language, skills and availability.
tandem.empty: No suggestions right now. Add more members or adjust availability.

safeguarding.title: Safeguarding Reports
safeguarding.description: Member-raised concerns about other members, coordinators, or organisations.

role_pack.title: Role presets
role_pack.description: Pre-configured permission sets for each caring community role. Install the full role pack to quickly set up your coordinators with the correct access. {{countLabel}}.

national_admin.title: National Admin
national_admin.description: Platform-wide oversight across all cantons and organisations.

canton_admin.title: Canton Admin
canton_admin.description: Manage all caring community activity within a canton.

municipality_admin.title: Municipality Admin
municipality_admin.description: Manage caring community activity within a municipality.

cooperative_coordinator.title: Cooperative Coordinator
cooperative_coordinator.description: Coordinate a local cooperative and its members.

organisation_coordinator.title: Organisation Coordinator
organisation_coordinator.description: Coordinate volunteers and partners for one organisation.

trusted_reviewer.title: Trusted Reviewer
trusted_reviewer.description: Approve hours quickly with light-touch oversight.

policy.title: Workflow policy
policy.description: Configure SLAs, approval rules, and reporting defaults. Changes take effect immediately on save and apply to all new hour submissions from that point forward.
policy.review_sla_days_description: Hours not reviewed within this many days will be flagged as overdue in the queue.
policy.escalation_sla_days_description: Overdue items not actioned within this many additional days will be escalated automatically.
policy.monthly_statement_day_description: Day of the month on which member statements are generated (e.g. 1 = 1st of each month).
policy.default_hour_value_chf_description: Used to calculate estimated social value on statements and municipal reports.
policy.approval_required_description: Require coordinator approval before hours are added to wallets.
policy.auto_approve_trusted_reviewers_descripti: Skip the queue for hours logged by trusted reviewers.
policy.allow_member_self_log_description: Let members log their own support hours without coordinator entry.
policy.require_organisation_for_partner_hours_d: Hours given on behalf of partners must be linked to an organisation.
policy.include_social_value_estimate_descriptio: Show estimated social value (CHF) on statements and reports.

member_statement.title: Member Statements
member_statement.description: Generate an hours-and-impact statement for a single member. Use this to share a formal record of their support hours, wallet balance, and estimated social value - useful for grant applications, reviews, or member queries. Enter a member ID and an optional date range, then preview or export CSV.
member_statement.empty: Enter a member ID and press Preview to view their statement.

signals.title: Coordinator signals
signals.description: A real-time snapshot of activity that may need coordinator attention - open requests, available offers, trusted organisations, and recent declines.

assisted_onboarding.title: Assisted onboarding
assisted_onboarding.description: Create an account on behalf of a member who can't self-register, then share their temporary password.
assisted_onboarding.password_note: Share this temporary password with the member. They will be asked to change it on first login.

invite_codes.title: Invite Codes
invite_codes.description: Generate a printable code that a new member can use to join without needing an email invitation.
invite_codes.label: Label (optional)
invite_codes.empty: No invite codes yet. Generate one above to share with a prospective member.

stages.title: Workflow stages
stages.description: The five-step caring community pipeline at a glance.

intake.title: Intake
intake.description: Welcome new members and capture their support needs.

match.title: Match
match.description: Pair supporters and recipients based on skills and availability.

log.title: Log Hours
log.description: Capture support hours as they happen.

verify.title: Verify
verify.description: Coordinators review and approve logged hours.

statement.title: Statement
statement.description: Issue monthly statements and impact reports.

predictive.title: Predictive Insights
predictive.subtitle: Forward-looking forecasts and proactive alerts. Spot regional care deficits before they become emergencies.
predictive.not_enough_activity: Not enough activity yet to forecast. Come back in a few weeks.
predictive.helper_churn_note: {{lapsed}} of {{prior}} previously active helpers haven't logged hours recently.
predictive.coefficient_drift_note: Categories with absolute drift > {{threshold}}% are flagged. Review baseline coefficients in the category coefficient editor.

recipients_without_tandem.title: Recipients without a regular supporter
recipients_without_tandem.message: These members received help in the last six months but have no active recurring tandem. Pair them with a steady supporter to strengthen continuity.

inactive_members.title: Previously active members have gone quiet
inactive_members.message: These members logged hours in the last six months but not in the last 30 days. A friendly check-in may help them re-engage.

overdue_reviews.title: Hour reviews have exceeded the service level
overdue_reviews.message: These pending hour logs are older than {{sla}} days. Approve or escalate them so members receive their credit.

coordinators_overloaded.title: Coordinators are overloaded
coordinators_overloaded.message: One or more coordinators have over 10 pending reviews. Reassign work before a bottleneck develops.

retention_dropping.title: Active member participation is declining
retention_dropping.message: This month's active member count ({{current}}) is below {{threshold_percent}}% of the recent {{months}}-month average ({{average}}). Consider a targeted outreach nudge.

overdue_check_ins.title: Tandem check-ins are overdue
overdue_check_ins.message: These active recurring relationships have passed their scheduled check-in. Contact both participants to confirm that they are well.

low_supply.title: Categories have unmet demand
low_supply.message: Active requests outnumber available offers in these categories. Highlight them to potential supporters.

relationships.title: Support relationships
relationships.description: Ongoing supporter-recipient pairs and recurring check-ins.
relationships.empty: No support relationships yet. Create one above.
relationships.log_validation_failed: Enter a valid date and 0.25-24 hours.

invite_card.subtitle: Neighbour-to-neighbour invitation
invite_card.instructions: Enter this code or visit the link below to join.

meta.title: Category Substitution Coefficients
meta.subtitle: How much each hour of community care substitutes for formal Spitex/Pflege hours. Used in Municipal Impact Report calculations.

about.title: About this page
about.body_prefix: Substitution Coefficients control how much social value each care category is worth in the cost-offset calculation. A coefficient of 1.0 means one hour equals one hour of formal Spitex/Pflege care (
about.body_middle: ). A coefficient of 0.5 means it substitutes for half an hour. A coefficient of 2.0 means it substitutes for two hours of formal care - for example, intensive personal care or specialist support. The
about.body_suffix: methodology recommends starting all categories at 1.0 and adjusting based on observed care intensity.
about.range_prefix: Values range from 0.00 (no care substitution value) to 9.99. Changes take effect on the next

migration.title: Migration pending
migration.body_middle: column has not been migrated yet. Run
migration.warning: A database migration is pending - save your changes after the migration completes to avoid them being overwritten.

meta.title: Commercial Boundary Map
meta.subtitle: AG82 - what is AGPL public, tenant-configurable, deployment-layer, or commercial

about.title: About this page
about.body_prefix: The Commercial Boundary defines which types of paid work are permitted within the community care programme and which must remain unpaid time credits. This boundary is a requirement of the
about.body_suffix: methodology and must be agreed with your municipal partner before the pilot launches. The matrix below lists activity categories and their permitted exchange type.
about.override_prefix: Use the per-row override to record a tenant-specific classification (for example, a private deployment that has a commercial bundle). Overrides are stored in tenant settings only - they never alter the canonical public classification shipped with the
about.override_suffix: repo. Once you confirm the boundary matrix, the agreement is recorded with a timestamp and your user ID, forming part of the pilot governance documentation that appears in the Pilot Launch Readiness check.

classification_descriptions.agpl_public: AGPL-licensed source code in the public repository. Anyone may deploy it under the licence terms.
classification_descriptions.tenant_config: AGPL-licensed functionality controlled by tenant configuration or a feature toggle.
classification_descriptions.private_deployment: An operational deployment layer such as infrastructure, build accounts, or signing-key ownership; this is not a code-licensing boundary.
classification_descriptions.commercial: A separately contracted service that is not included in the public AGPL package.

caring_community_module.label: Caring Community module
caring_community_module.description: The umbrella feature flag that activates the complete Caring Community workflow for a tenant.
caring_community_module.notes: This is the master gate for the other caring features.

caring_help_requests.label: Help requests
caring_help_requests.description: The caring help-request workflow, including caregiver requests made on another person's behalf, matching, and acceptance.

caring_support_relationships.label: Support relationships
caring_support_relationships.description: Long-running caring relationships between a recipient and one or more supporters, with recurring check-ins.

caring_caregiver_links.label: Caregiver links
caring_caregiver_links.description: A verified link between an informal caregiver and the person they support, allowing requests to be made on that person's behalf.

caring_substitute_cover.label: Substitute and cover scheduling
caring_substitute_cover.description: Find substitute caregivers when the primary supporter is unavailable, with conflict detection and confirmation.

caring_warmth_pass.label: Warmth Pass
caring_warmth_pass.description: A recipient-controlled dignity layer that governs who may see their needs and supports consent-based introductions.

caring_emergency_alerts.label: Emergency alerts
caring_emergency_alerts.description: Emergency broadcasts from a recipient or caregiver to a vetted radius of trusted neighbours.

caring_sub_regions.label: Caring sub-regions
caring_sub_regions.description: Regional cells within a tenant, such as districts or neighbourhoods, with their own coordinator and KPI roll-up.

caring_research_consent.label: Research consent flag
caring_research_consent.description: A member opt-in for inclusion in anonymised research datasets shared with academic or municipal partners.

caring_municipal_roi.label: Municipal return-on-investment report
caring_municipal_roi.description: A report of formal-care cost offsets and prevention value for public-sector procurement discussions.

caring_pilot_scoreboard.label: Pilot scoreboard
caring_pilot_scoreboard.description: A live KPI pack for pilot stand-ups and funder-facing progress reviews.

caring_disclosure_pack.label: FADP/nDSG disclosure pack
caring_disclosure_pack.description: An editable Swiss data-protection disclosure pack for legal review before resident onboarding.

caring_operating_policy.label: Operating policy workshop
caring_operating_policy.description: A schema-driven policy covering approval authority, service levels, legacy-hour settlement, rates, and review cadence.

safeguarding_reports.label: Safeguarding reports
safeguarding_reports.description: A member-to-coordinator safeguarding concern workflow with an audit trail and escalation routing.

trust_tier_system.label: Trust tier system
trust_tier_system.description: Member trust progression that controls which caring actions a new, trusted, or vetted member may perform.

municipal_verification.label: Municipal verification badge
municipal_verification.description: An optional municipal verification marker on a member profile, based on residence or background checks.

xp_badges_journeys.label: XP, badges, and journeys
xp_badges_journeys.description: An engagement layer with XP, badges, journeys, and challenges that recognise caring participation.

appreciation_messages.label: Appreciation messages
appreciation_messages.description: A lightweight thank-you flow that lets recipients acknowledge supporters publicly.

local_advertising_campaigns.label: Local advertising campaigns
local_advertising_campaigns.description: In-app placements sold to local merchants. The code is AGPL-licensed, participation is tenant-controlled, and revenue stays with the deployer.
local_advertising_campaigns.notes: Opt-in per tenant; the public code does not impose a revenue share with the platform operator.

paid_push_campaigns.label: Paid push campaigns
paid_push_campaigns.description: Targeted push-notification campaigns that merchants may purchase to reach opted-in members.
paid_push_campaigns.notes: The tenant must enable the feature and accept responsibility for push-delivery costs.

premium_member_tier.label: Premium member tier
premium_member_tier.description: An optional paid membership tier with additional benefits, operated at the tenant's discretion.

merchant_loyalty_coupons.label: Merchant loyalty and coupons
merchant_loyalty_coupons.description: Local merchant loyalty cards and coupon redemption linked to time-credit balances.

verein_dues_collection.label: Association dues
verein_dues_collection.description: Recurring association-dues collection through Stripe, with member statements and reconciliation.
verein_dues_collection.notes: The Stripe account is operational infrastructure; the application code remains AGPL-licensed.

smart_matching_engine.label: Smart matching engine
smart_matching_engine.description: A heuristic matcher that pairs help requests with likely supporters using skills, distance, and history.

ki_agenten_framework.label: KI-Agenten framework
ki_agenten_framework.description: A per-tenant agent runtime that lets coordinators define structured assistants for repeatable workflows.
ki_agenten_framework.notes: The code is AGPL-licensed; each tenant supplies its own language-model API credentials and pays the provider directly.

ai_chat_assistant.label: AI chat assistant
ai_chat_assistant.description: A member-facing assistant grounded in the tenant knowledge base for help and onboarding.
ai_chat_assistant.notes: Each tenant supplies its own OpenAI API credentials.

embedding_recommendations.label: Embedding-based recommendations
embedding_recommendations.description: Embedding models power listing, member, and group recommendations across the platform.
embedding_recommendations.notes: Each tenant is responsible for its embedding-provider usage costs.

tenant_branded_native_app.label: Tenant-branded native app
tenant_branded_native_app.description: An iOS and Android Capacitor app published under the tenant's brand. The source is AGPL-licensed; the release infrastructure is tenant-owned.
tenant_branded_native_app.notes: Apple and Google developer accounts, signing keys, build pipelines, and store-review workflows are operational responsibilities outside the package.

paid_regional_analytics.label: Paid regional analytics
paid_regional_analytics.description: Cross-tenant aggregate analytics for regional and municipal procurement, offered separately to public-sector buyers.
paid_regional_analytics.notes: This is a separate public-sector product that requires a commercial agreement with the platform operator.

partner_api_access.label: Partner API access
partner_api_access.description: A rate-limited API for research, government, and integration partners that may include cross-tenant aggregate queries.
partner_api_access.notes: A commercial agreement is required; this access is not enabled for AGPL deployers by default.

export.generated: Generated {{date}} from the NEXUS admin panel.

toasts.load_failed: Failed to load commercial boundary map

meta.title: Pilot Data Quality
meta.subtitle: AG84 - readiness checks before onboarding real residents

about.title: About this page
about.body: Data Quality checks scan your platform data for issues that could distort pilot metrics or cause reporting errors - for example, members with duplicate profiles, hours logged against deleted listings, or exchange records missing required fields. Run this monthly before generating reports. Resolve all Critical and Warning items before submitting reports to funders or municipal partners.

severity_guide.title: Severity guide
severity_guide.danger: Errors that will cause incorrect numbers in reports - must fix before exporting.
severity_guide.warning: Potential issues that may affect metric accuracy - review and confirm or fix.
severity_guide.ok: No issues detected in this category.
severity_guide.note_suffix: on any card to see the specific records. Some issues can be corrected in the platform; others require manual review of the underlying data.

drilldown.subtitle: Up to 50 affected rows - resolve or document before launch.

duplicate_emails.label: Duplicate email addresses
duplicate_emails.schema_unavailable: The users table or email column is not available in this tenant schema.
duplicate_emails.found: Multiple users share the same email address. Merge or remove duplicate accounts before launch.
duplicate_emails.clear: No duplicate email addresses were detected.

duplicate_phones.label: Duplicate phone numbers
duplicate_phones.schema_unavailable: The users table or phone column is not available in this tenant schema.
duplicate_phones.found: Several members share the same phone number. Confirm whether they are different residents.
duplicate_phones.clear: No duplicate phone numbers were detected.

missing_preferred_language.label: Members without a preferred language
missing_preferred_language.schema_unavailable: The preferred-language field is not available in this tenant schema.
missing_preferred_language.found: Some members have not selected a preferred language. Set one before sending resident communications.
missing_preferred_language.clear: Every member has a preferred language.

missing_sub_region.label: Members without a sub-region
missing_sub_region.regions_unavailable: Sub-regions are not available in this tenant schema, so this check does not apply.
missing_sub_region.column_unavailable: Member sub-region assignments are not available in this tenant schema, so this check does not apply.
missing_sub_region.no_regions: No sub-regions have been configured, so this check does not apply.
missing_sub_region.found: Members without a sub-region will not appear in neighbourhood-scoped feeds.
missing_sub_region.clear: Every member is assigned to a sub-region.

missing_coordinator_assignment.label: Caring relationships without a coordinator
missing_coordinator_assignment.table_unavailable: Caring support relationships are not available in this tenant schema.
missing_coordinator_assignment.column_unavailable: Coordinator assignments are not available in this tenant schema.
missing_coordinator_assignment.found: Assign a coordinator to every active caring relationship before launch.
missing_coordinator_assignment.clear: Every caring relationship has a coordinator.

unverified_organisations.label: Unverified organisations
unverified_organisations.table_unavailable: Volunteer organisations are not available in this tenant schema.
unverified_organisations.verification_unavailable: Organisation verification fields are not available in this tenant schema.
unverified_organisations.found: Approve or reject every pending organisation so members can trust its listings.

seed_marker_users.label: Demo or seed accounts
seed_marker_users.table_unavailable: Members are not available in this tenant schema.
seed_marker_users.found: Demo or seed accounts are still present. Remove them before onboarding real residents.
seed_marker_users.clear: No demo or seed accounts were detected.

unanswered_help_requests.label: Unanswered help requests older than 30 days
unanswered_help_requests.table_unavailable: Caring help requests are not available in this tenant schema.
unanswered_help_requests.found: Old pending help requests undermine trust. Close, decline, or match every one.
unanswered_help_requests.clear: There are no stale help requests.

members_without_role.label: Members without a role
members_without_role.schema_unavailable: Member roles are not available in this tenant schema.
members_without_role.found: Assign every member a role so their permissions resolve correctly.

tenant_setting_completeness.label: Tenant settings completeness
tenant_setting_completeness.table_unavailable: Tenant settings are not available in this tenant schema.
tenant_setting_completeness.found: One or more pre-launch settings are missing. Review the AG80 disclosure pack and AG81 operating policy.
tenant_setting_completeness.clear: Every required pre-launch tenant setting is configured.

notes.drilldown_not_available: A row-level drill-down is not available for this check.

meta.title: FADP / nDSG Disclosure Pack
meta.subtitle: AG80 - pilot disclosure document for Swiss data-protection law

about.title: About this page
about.body_suffix: data-protection disclosure document that residents must be made aware of before participating in the community care programme. It covers the data controller and processor identities, data categories processed, lawful basis, data-subject rights, incident response contacts, and cross-border transfer safeguards.
about.review_prefix: This pack is a working draft - review it with
about.review_middle: counsel before publishing. Edit the controller details, incident-response owner, and isolated-node configuration for your deployment, then use
about.review_suffix: to produce the legal handover document. Defaults reflect platform-side commitments. Save any changes before exporting.

isolated_node.description_prefix: These fields are only relevant if this deployment runs on canton-controlled infrastructure. For standard hosted deployments, leave as defaults. Each owner field identifies the legal entity responsible for that infrastructure component under

descriptions.hosting_owner: Legal entity that controls the server infrastructure (e.g. the canton IT department).
descriptions.smtp_owner: Legal entity responsible for outbound email delivery.
descriptions.storage_owner: Legal entity controlling file/blob storage (uploads, exports).
descriptions.backup_owner: Legal entity responsible for database backup storage and retention.
descriptions.update_cadence: How frequently the canton node receives platform updates (e.g. 'monthly', 'quarterly').

default_copy.isolated_node_description: Canton-controlled deployment with its own email delivery, storage, and backups. Federation can be disabled entirely.
default_copy.fadp_authority: Federal Data Protection and Information Commissioner (FDPIC / EDÖB)
default_copy.safeguard_swiss_us_framework: Swiss–US Data Privacy Framework, where applicable

values.name: name
values.no_personal_data_shared_outside_tenant: No personal data shared outside the tenant

meta.title: External Integration Backlog
meta.subtitle: AG87 - partner-dependent integrations awaiting external owner / DSA / sandbox

about.title: About this page
about.body: Partner Integration Tracker is a backlog tracker for partner-dependent connections - banking, AHV submission, postal address verification, and cantonal data feeds. Each entry tracks the integration owner, data sharing agreement (DSA) status, and sandbox readiness. Use this to coordinate with external partners and report progress to stakeholders.
about.dsa_body: Must be signed before any live data exchange. Draft agreements using the Research Partnerships templates.
about.sandbox_body: Link to the partner's test environment. Validate the integration in sandbox before promoting to live.
about.statuses_body: Proposed -> Scoping -> Sandbox -> Live. Blocked means an external dependency is unresolved.

empty.title: No integrations tracked yet
empty.body: Seed a curated set of well-known partner-dependent integrations (AHV submission, Spitex handoff, cantonal master-data feed, PostFinance, Twint, postal-address verification) so the platform operator can track owner, DSA status, and sandbox readiness.

ahv_submission_gateway.name: AHV submission gateway
ahv_submission_gateway.notes: Official channel for AHV-relevant volunteer-hour reports. The canonical submission interface still needs to be confirmed.

spitex_handoff.name: Spitex care-coordination handoff
spitex_handoff.notes: Two-way handoff with cantonal Spitex providers for care-recipient circles. A data-sharing agreement and interface specification are needed from each provider.

cantonal_master_data.name: Cantonal master-data feed
cantonal_master_data.notes: Subscribed address and household data from the cantonal registry to keep care-recipient profiles current.

postfinance_payment.name: PostFinance payment integration
postfinance_payment.notes: Swiss banking partner for cash-out and treasury operations. A merchant agreement is required.

twint_payment.name: Twint payment
twint_payment.notes: Twint acceptance for membership fees and donations. Merchant onboarding through a partner bank is required.

postal_address_verification.name: Postal-address verification
postal_address_verification.notes: Address normalisation and validation against the Swiss Post directory.

editor.name: Name
editor.notes_placeholder: Open questions, blockers, dependencies, contractual notes...

delete_modal.title: Remove integration
delete_modal.body_prefix: This will permanently remove the backlog entry for
delete_modal.body_suffix: The external partner record is not affected - only the tracking entry is removed.

meta.title: Partner Cooperatives
meta.subtitle: Connect with other cooperatives so members can send hours between communities.

about.title: About this page
about.body: Partner Cooperatives are other NEXUS cooperatives that your community has agreed to share members and hours with. Once a peer connection is established, members can transfer hours between communities and view federated listings. Each peer connection requires a shared secret that both sides must configure - contact the peer cooperative's administrator to exchange credentials.
about.shared_secret_body: A private key both cooperatives use to prove who they are to each other. It must be entered exactly the same on both sides. Replace it every 6-12 months, and straight away if you think anyone else has seen it.
about.pending_body: Credentials set, awaiting first successful API call.
about.suspended_body: Last sync failed or manually suspended - check that the peer's base URL is correct and reachable.

peers.title: Registered Peers
peers.empty: No federation peers yet - add one to start.

create_modal.title: Add Federation Peer
create_modal.peer_slug_description: Lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens. Used as destination cooperative slug for outbound transfers.
create_modal.base_url_description: HTTPS URL of the remote NEXUS API. The /v2/federation/hour-transfer/inbound path is appended automatically.
create_modal.notes_placeholder: Internal notes - partner contact, agreement reference...
create_modal.status_hint_middle: After the remote side has registered the same shared secret, set both sides to

secret_modal.title: One-Time Shared Secret
secret_modal.body_suffix: Copy it now and paste it into the remote NEXUS install's federation peer entry for this cooperative. If you lose it, you must rotate the secret here and update the remote side again.

validation.required_fields: All required fields must be filled.

federation_peers_admin.confirm_rotate: Rotate the shared secret for "{{name}}"? The old secret stops working immediately and you must update the remote side.
federation_peers_admin.confirm_delete: Delete federation peer "{{name}}"? This is permanent.

about.title: About this page
about.body: Service Level Agreements (SLAs) set the maximum expected response and resolution times for help requests. When a request approaches or exceeds its SLA, it is flagged as overdue in this dashboard. SLAs help you maintain consistent service quality and generate accurate pilot metrics.
about.first_response_prefix: time from request submission to first coordinator contact (status moves out of
about.resolution_prefix: time from first response to the request being matched and confirmed (status reaches
about.note: SLA windows are set in the AG81 Operating Policy. Edit them using the "Edit policy" button above. Set tighter SLAs for urgent care categories (e.g. medical transport) and looser ones for social activities.

policy.first_response_description: - time from submission to first coordinator contact
policy.resolution_description: - time from first response to request being matched and confirmed
policy.platform_defaults: Platform defaults - no AG81 workshop run

open.title: Open help requests
open.subtitle: {{count}} open - sorted breached first
open.empty_bucket: No open help requests in this bucket.

footer.proxy_prefix: proxy: first-response = status moves out of

meta.title: Developer Integration Reference
meta.subtitle: A single-page reference of every public API, webhook, federation endpoint, and OAuth surface available in this deployment - ready to hand to a technical integration partner.

about.title: About this page
about.body: Use this page when setting up an integration with a third-party service, a municipality's data system, or a federated NEXUS network. Every endpoint listed here is live and scoped to this deployment. Sample payloads are illustrative - real values depend on your tenant slug and partner credentials. Share this URL with integration partners rather than pointing them to the raw API docs.

sections.read_full_spec: Read the full spec on GitHub

openapi.title: OpenAPI specification
openapi.body: A machine-readable specification for every public API surface, generated from the controllers shipped with this platform.

partner_api.title: Partner API v1
partner_api.body: An OAuth-secured API for third-party integrators, covering members, listings, wallet credit, community aggregates, and webhook subscriptions.

oauth.title: OAuth client credentials
oauth.body: The standard OAuth 2.0 client-credentials flow for trusted partner servers, including token revocation.

webhooks.title: Webhook subscriptions
webhooks.body: Partners subscribe to event topics. Each payload is signed with HMAC-SHA256 over the complete body using that subscription's secret.

federation.title: Federation aggregates
federation.body: Each tenant exposes a read-only signed aggregate endpoint. Payloads contain counts, brackets, top categories, and locales, but never raw personal data.

sample_payloads.title: Sample payloads
sample_payloads.body: Representative payloads for an integration partner. These examples are illustrative and do not contain live tenant data.

partner_checklist.title: What an integration partner receives
partner_checklist.body: Share this checklist with a prospective integration partner before implementation begins.

verification_notes.webhook_signature: Verify the X-NEXUS-Signature header by computing HMAC-SHA256 over the complete request body with the subscription secret, then compare the signatures in constant time. Reject payloads older than the configured replay window, which defaults to five minutes.

checklist.oauth_credentials: An OAuth client ID and client secret dedicated to this partner
checklist.allowed_scopes: The approved scopes, such as read:users, read:listings, and write:wallet
checklist.webhook_subscription: A webhook subscription, HMAC secret, and endpoint URL allow-list
checklist.openapi_spec: The OpenAPI specification URL in JSON or YAML format
checklist.sandbox_tenant: A sandbox tenant slug for integration testing
checklist.federation_public_key: The federation aggregate signing public key, when the partner participates in federation
checklist.rate_limit_headers: Documentation for the X-RateLimit-Limit and X-RateLimit-Remaining headers
checklist.data_sharing_agreement: A draft data-sharing agreement and a named data protection contact
checklist.incident_runbook: The incident-response runbook URL from the AG80 disclosure pack

meta.title: Isolated-Node Decision Gate
meta.subtitle: AG85 - readiness checklist for canton-controlled deployment

about.title: About this page
about.body_middle: on infrastructure controlled entirely by the canton or municipality - no data leaves the canton boundary. This configuration is required for
about.body_suffix: deployments under strict Swiss cantonal data protection rules. Most communities run as standard hosted deployments and do not need to configure this section.
about.workflow: Work through each decision item below: set the value, assign an owner, and mark it as Decided when agreed. The gate closes (and the Pilot Launch Readiness check passes) once every item is in Decided status.

warning.title: Important - only configure if required
warning.body_prefix: Only change these settings if your municipality has explicitly specified an isolated-node requirement. Changing the deployment mode after pilot launch requires coordination with the
warning.body_suffix: technical team. Do not change this setting without agreement from your cantonal data protection officer.

deployment_mode.label: Deployment mode
deployment_mode.help: Choose whether this is a shared hosted tenant, a custom-domain hosted tenant, or a fully isolated node controlled by the regional authority.

hosting_owner.label: Hosting owner
hosting_owner.help: Name the organisation responsible for the node's servers, domain, and TLS configuration.

smtp_owner.label: SMTP and outbound email owner
smtp_owner.help: Name the organisation responsible for outbound email delivery and its mail provider account.

storage_owner.label: Storage owner
storage_owner.help: Name the organisation responsible for file uploads, attachments, and persistent object storage.

backup_owner.label: Backup owner
backup_owner.help: Name the organisation responsible for daily backups, retention, and tested database restores.

update_cadence.label: Update cadence
update_cadence.help: Choose how often the node receives upstream NEXUS source updates.

source_release_workflow.label: Source release workflow
source_release_workflow.help: Describe how AGPL source updates reach the isolated node, including repository mirrors, signed tags, and manual review.

telemetry_default.label: Telemetry default
telemetry_default.help: Choose the default state for outbound telemetry and error reporting on this node.

federation_key_exchange.label: Federation key exchange
federation_key_exchange.help: Describe whether and how this node federates with regional peers, including key custody and exchange protocol.

dpo_appointed.label: Data protection officer
dpo_appointed.help: Record the named data protection officer and their contact details for the isolated deployment.

incident_runbook_url.label: Incident runbook URL
incident_runbook_url.help: Link to the operational runbook for downtime, breaches, key compromise, and restore drills.

choices.hosted_custom_domain: Hosted tenant with a custom domain

fields.owner_description: Person or organisation responsible for this decision
fields.notes_description: Context, links, contract references, or blockers

modal.subtitle: Update value, owner, status, and notes for this gate item

meta.title: Community KPI Baselines
meta.subtitle_prefix: Capture snapshots of your community metrics to track before/after

about.title: About this page
about.body_prefix: Baselines record the state of your community before the Caring Community programme begins. They are compared against current metrics at each quarterly review to demonstrate impact. Capture baselines for every
about.body_suffix: before onboarding residents - once the pilot starts, the baseline is frozen and cannot be backdated.
about.compare_suffix: on any baseline row to see a side-by-side comparison against current platform data. Metrics showing 25%+ change on key impact indicators are highlighted as Impact achieved.

fields.baseline_description: A short name to identify this snapshot (e.g. "Q1 2025 Pre-launch")
fields.notes_placeholder: e.g. 'GP referral data Q1 2026, member survey March 2026, captured before platform rollout to all Gemeinden'
fields.notes_description: Document where these numbers come from (e.g. 'GP referral data Q1 2026', 'member survey March 2026'). Cited sources make the baseline defensible in grant reporting.

modal.body: This will take a live snapshot of your community metrics right now and save it as a named baseline for future comparisons.
modal.metrics_captured: Metrics captured: active member count, approved volunteer hours (last 90 days), active support relationships, distinct recipients, and total exchanges.

toasts.saved: Baseline saved. Use it to compare future metrics against this snapshot.

meta.title: Lead Nurture
meta.subtitle: Track and nurture prospective partners - municipalities, investors, businesses, residents, and community organisations - from first contact through to onboarding.

about.title: About this page
about.body: The Lead Nurture tracker is a lightweight CRM for NEXUS deployment opportunities. When a municipality, potential funder, or business partner expresses interest, add them here and track their progress through the funnel. Use the stage field to record where each contact is in the conversation, and the follow-up date to ensure no one goes cold. Export to CSV for reporting or import into an external CRM.

filters.title: Filters
filters.segment_description: municipality: local gov; investor: funder; business: sponsorship; resident: individual; partner: NGO/charity.

contacts.title: Contacts ({{count}})
contacts.empty: No contacts match the current filters.

modal.title: Edit contact
modal.stage_description: captured: first contact; contacted: reached out; engaged: active conversation; qualified: interest and budget confirmed; converted: signed up; dormant: gone quiet; unsubscribed: opted out.

toasts.load_failed: Failed to load lead nurture contacts

meta.title: Municipal Communication Copilot
meta.subtitle: Draft official announcements, have them polished by AI, check tone and clarity, then publish. Designed for coordinators who need to communicate with residents and municipality contacts in a consistent, accessible voice.

about.title: About this page
about.body: The Municipal Copilot helps you write announcements that are clear, appropriately formal, and free of jargon. Paste a rough draft - the AI will suggest improvements, flag tone issues (too formal, condescending, or unclear), and list any moderation concerns. You review and accept or reject the suggestion before anything is published.

fields.draft_placeholder: Paste a rough draft - the copilot will polish, check tone, and flag issues.
fields.audience_description: Specify who this announcement is for (e.g. 'all residents', 'volunteers', 'municipality contacts') so the AI can calibrate the tone and vocabulary appropriately.
fields.reason_placeholder: Why is this proposal being rejected?

states.accepted: Accepted but not published yet. Press Publish to send it as an announcement.

empty.no_latest: No proposal yet. Generate one from the draft on the left.
empty.no_proposals: No proposals yet. Drafts you analyse will appear here as an audit trail.

toasts.accepted: Proposal accepted and published as an announcement
toasts.publish_failed: Proposal accepted, but publishing failed - press Publish to retry

meta.title: Municipal Impact Report
meta.subtitle: Evidence for B2G procurement - care cost offsets and social capital metrics

about.title: About this page
about.body_middle: Report calculates the social and economic value of your community care programme using the
about.body_suffix: Caring Community methodology. It shows the estimated cost offset to formal care services (Spitex/Pflege), the social value generated, and trend data across reporting periods. This report is designed to be shared with municipal partners and funders as evidence of the programme's impact.
about.formula_prefix: Cost offset = total approved care hours x
about.formula_suffix: hourly rate x prevention multiplier. The prevention multiplier (default: 2.0) reflects that one hour of informal care prevents approximately two hours of formal care intervention, based on Caring Community research.

methodology.default_source: Swiss formal care assistant rate, SECO 2024
methodology.prevention_value_prefix: Prevention value applies a {{multiplier}}x multiplier per
methodology.default_prevention_prefix: Prevention value applies a 2x multiplier per
methodology.substitution_applied: Hours are weighted by per-category substitution coefficients.

stats.weighted_hours_tooltip: Hours are weighted by category-specific substitution rates (e.g. companionship 0.4x, transport 0.7x, errands 1.0x) per Caring Community methodology.

roi.title: Estimated Care Cost Offset
roi.prevented_this_period: in formal care costs prevented this period
roi.social_isolation_supported: People supported out of social isolation

municipalities.title: What this means for municipalities
municipalities.point_hour_prefix: Every hour of community caring time saves approximately
municipalities.point_prevention: Preventative community support can reduce residential care needs - estimated 2x multiplier
municipalities.point_nexus: makes this impact measurable, auditable, and reportable for cantonal and municipal procurement
municipalities.point_export: CSV export available - use the button in the page header for Caring Community, Pro Senectute, and cantonal social department reporting.

breakdown.title: Breakdown by sub-region
breakdown.description: Sub-region breakdown shows which neighbourhoods are generating the most care activity. Use this to identify areas that may need more coordinator support or volunteer recruitment.

toasts.load_failed: Failed to load municipal impact data

meta.title: Municipal verification
meta.description: Mark this community as a verified municipal partner. Adds a 'Verified municipality' badge to public reports.

about.title: About this page
about.body: Municipal Verification records the formal sign-off from a municipality or canton partner that this community care programme meets their requirements. Verification is required for the Pilot Launch Readiness check and unlocks the Municipal Impact Report. Use DNS TXT verification for official municipal domains, or apply an admin attestation if DNS control is not available.
about.pending_dns: Verification token generated - awaiting DNS propagation and confirmation.
about.verified: Formally verified - the municipal reporting pack is unlocked.
about.revoked: Verification was removed - re-verify to restore access.

current.title: Current status
current.not_verified: This tenant is not currently verified. Use the options below to start a DNS verification or apply a manual admin attestation.

request.title: Request verification
request.dns_body: Provide the official municipality domain you control. We'll generate a DNS TXT record for you to publish; once it propagates, the system marks this tenant as verified.
request.attestation_body: Use this only when DNS control is not available. The verification is recorded as manually attested by an admin.

fields.attestation_description: Why are you attesting this without DNS verification? (Audit trail.)

dns.publish_record: Publish this TXT record on your DNS, then wait for propagation:

revoke_modal.title: Revoke verification?
revoke_modal.body_suffix: This removes the verified badge and the verification record will be marked as revoked.

meta.title: Pilot Launch Readiness
meta.subtitle: AG95 - go/no-go report combining AG80-AG87 evaluation surfaces

about.title: About this page
about.body: This is the go/no-go gate before your Caring Community pilot goes live. Each section below corresponds to a configuration or evaluation module (AG80-AG87). All sections must reach Ready status before the Launch button is enabled. Once launched, this is a one-way action - the launch timestamp and operator are recorded permanently.
about.review_help: Sections showing Needs review have missing or incomplete configuration. Click through to the relevant module to resolve the listed items.

disclosure_pack.not_started: The pack still uses platform defaults and has no named controller.
disclosure_pack.needs_review: The controller, data protection officer, or incident contact is incomplete.
disclosure_pack.ready: The controller, data protection officer, and incident contact are recorded.

operating_policy.not_started: The policy still uses platform defaults. Schedule the policy workshop.
operating_policy.needs_review: The workshop is complete, but the appendix URL or safeguarding owner is missing.
operating_policy.ready: The policy workshop is complete, the appendix is linked, and a safeguarding owner is assigned.

commercial_boundary.needs_review: Default classifications are in effect, but a coordinator has not acknowledged the matrix.
commercial_boundary.ready_with_overrides: {{count}} override is applied and the matrix has been reviewed.
commercial_boundary.ready_with_overrides_one: {{count}} override is applied and the matrix has been reviewed.
commercial_boundary.ready_with_overrides_other: {{count}} overrides are applied and the matrix has been reviewed.
commercial_boundary.ready_default: The default matrix has been acknowledged.

pilot_scoreboard.not_started: No pre-pilot baseline has been captured, so a before-and-after comparison is not yet possible.
pilot_scoreboard.needs_review: The pre-pilot baseline is captured, but the quarterly review is overdue.
pilot_scoreboard.ready: The pre-pilot baseline is captured and the quarterly review cadence is on track.

data_quality.blocked: {{count}} blocking issue remains. Duplicate accounts or seed users are still present.
data_quality.blocked_one: {{count}} blocking issue remains. Duplicate accounts or seed users are still present.
data_quality.blocked_other: {{count}} blocking issues remain. Duplicate accounts or seed users are still present.
data_quality.ready: Every check passes. The data is ready for real residents.

isolated_node.hosted_closed: The gate is closed. It remains informational because this deployment is hosted.
isolated_node.hosted_informational: The gate is not required for hosted deployments and remains informational.
isolated_node.blocked: {{count}} blocked decision prevents the isolated deployment from launching.
isolated_node.blocked_one: {{count}} blocked decision prevents the isolated deployment from launching.
isolated_node.blocked_other: {{count}} blocked decisions prevent the isolated deployment from launching.
isolated_node.needs_review: {{decided}} of {{total}} decisions are recorded. An isolated deployment requires every decision to be complete.
isolated_node.ready: Every gate decision is recorded. The isolated deployment is ready.

external_integrations.not_started: The backlog is empty. Seed the defaults or confirm that no partner integrations are required.
external_integrations.blocked: {{count}} integration is blocked, so partner-dependent features cannot launch.
external_integrations.blocked_one: {{count}} integration is blocked, so partner-dependent features cannot launch.
external_integrations.blocked_other: {{count}} integrations are blocked, so partner-dependent features cannot launch.
external_integrations.ready: {{count}} integration item is tracked and none are blocked.
external_integrations.ready_one: {{count}} integration item is tracked and none are blocked.
external_integrations.ready_other: {{count}} integration items are tracked and none are blocked.

overall_summaries.blocked: One or more sections are blocked, so launching the pilot is not safe.
overall_summaries.ready: Every section is ready and the pilot may launch.
overall_summaries.needs_review: {{ready}} of {{total}} sections are ready. A coordinator review is required before launch.
overall_summaries.not_started: {{ready}} of {{total}} sections are ready. The end-to-end pilot evaluation has not been completed.

states.launched_body: Launched on {{date}}{{user}}. This is a one-way milestone - readiness checks remain visible for audit.
states.ready_body_prefix: Every readiness section is ready. Click
states.ready_body_suffix: when your coordinator team is ready to make this a one-way decision.
states.blocked_body: Resolve every section below before the launch button is enabled.

isolated_node.required: Isolated-node gate is active - this deployment is configured as a canton-isolated node (AG85). All items must reach Decided status before launch.
isolated_node.informational: Isolated-node gate is informational for standard hosted deployments. It only blocks launch when AG85 deployment_mode is set to canton_isolated_node. If your municipality has not specified an isolated-node requirement, you can disregard items in this section.

timestamps.report_generated: Report generated {{date}}. Click any section to open the corresponding admin surface.

modal.title: Launch the pilot?
modal.body_suffix: It records the launch timestamp and operator on the tenant, and platform reports begin treating the community as live.
modal.confirm_intro: Before continuing, confirm with your coordinator team that:
modal.check_disclosure: The disclosure pack, operating policy, and commercial boundary are signed off
modal.check_baseline: The pre-pilot scoreboard baseline has been captured
modal.check_residents: Real residents are ready to be invited

toasts.cannot_launch: Cannot launch - readiness gate is not closed.
toasts.already_launched: This pilot has already been launched.

errors.load_failed: The launch-readiness report could not be loaded.
errors.unexpected_response: The launch-readiness report returned an unexpected response.

meta.title: Pilot Success Scoreboard
meta.subtitle: AG83 - pre-pilot baseline + 90-day rolling metrics with quarterly review cadence

about.title: About this page
about.body: All metrics use a 90-day rolling window. Capture a pre-pilot baseline before onboarding real residents, then run a quarterly review snapshot every 3 months. The comparison table shows how each metric has moved relative to that baseline.
about.methodology_prefix: Cost offset is calculated using the
about.methodology_middle: methodology: hours of informal care x
about.methodology_suffix: 35/hr (Swiss formal-care assistant rate) x 2 (prevention multiplier reflecting avoided formal care costs). This methodology is recognised by Swiss cantonal social services for pilot reporting.

sections.no_baseline: No pre-pilot baseline yet - current values shown alone

pre_modal.title: Capture pre-pilot baseline
pre_modal.body: Capture this ONCE before onboarding real residents. It freezes the current 90-day metrics as your comparison baseline. All future quarterly reviews will calculate delta against this snapshot. This action cannot be undone.
pre_modal.notes_placeholder: Pilot region, participating municipality, decisions made before launch...

quarterly_modal.title: Capture quarterly review
quarterly_modal.body: Run every 3 months after pilot launch. The snapshot label defaults to the current quarter (e.g. Q2_2026) but you can customise it. Add notes to record what changed since the last review - milestones reached, policy changes, resident feedback. Snapshot covers the most recent 90-day window from today.
quarterly_modal.label_description: Use a recognisable label such as Q2_2026 or 2026_Q3. Defaults to quarterly_YYYY_MM.
quarterly_modal.notes_placeholder: What changed this quarter, milestones reached...

meta.title: Research Partnerships
meta.subtitle: Manage privacy-safe research partners, aggregate dataset exports, and revocation history

about.title: About this page
about.body: Research partnerships give approved academic or policy researchers controlled access to anonymised platform data for evaluation purposes. Each partnership defines a specific data scope, a duration, and a data sharing agreement. All access is logged. Partners never see individual member data - only aggregated or anonymised datasets.
about.data_scope_body: Define exactly what data the researcher can access, for example aggregate monthly care hours by sub-region, excluding all personal identifiers. The scope is contractually binding and audited quarterly.
about.download_history_body: Every data export is logged with a timestamp and the researcher's details. This log is available to your Data Protection Officer on request. Use Revoke to withdraw a previously issued dataset.

export_modal.title: Generate Aggregate Dataset
export_modal.privacy_note: Exports are aggregate only and use suppression thresholds. Direct member identifiers are not included.

meta.title: Smart member nudges
meta.description: Automated suggestions that nudge members toward high-fit tandem partners. Conservative defaults - runs on a daily cap.

about.title: About this page
about.body: Smart Nudges are automated prompts sent to members when a high-fit tandem-match partner is detected. Members who would benefit from a peer connection are identified by the matching engine and sent an in-app notification (and optionally email) suggesting they connect. Nudges respect a cooldown window so no member is contacted too frequently. Use the dry-run to preview who would be nudged before dispatching for real.
about.inactivity_body: triggered when a member hasn't logged in for the configured number of days
about.unmatched_request_body: triggered when a help request has no offers after the cooldown window passes
about.follow_up_body: sent to the coordinator after an approved exchange hasn't been marked complete

config.title: Configuration
config.engine_description: When off, no nudges are dispatched.
config.min_score_description: Tandem-match score threshold for sending a nudge
config.cooldown_description: Wait this long before nudging the same pair again
config.daily_limit_description: Maximum nudges per day across the whole tenant
config.save_note: Changes take effect immediately - the next scheduled nudge run will apply the new settings.

dispatch_modal.title: Dispatch nudges now?
dispatch_modal.body_suffix: nudge notifications to eligible members right now. Make sure you've reviewed a dry-run first.

toasts.config_saved: Nudge config saved. The updated rules will apply on the next scheduled run.

meta.title: Sub-Regions
meta.subtitle: Quartier, Ortsteil, Gemeinde and Kanton subdivisions for the care provider directory and locality filters

about.title: About this page
about.body: Sub-Regions divide your community into geographical areas (neighbourhoods, districts, or wards). They are used to assign coordinators to specific areas, filter help requests by location, and break down pilot metrics by neighbourhood. Adding sub-regions is optional but strongly recommended for larger communities with multiple coordinator teams.
about.postal_codes_note: For the Postal codes field: enter a postcode list, neighbourhood name, or freeform description. Precise boundaries are not required - this is for coordinator assignment and reporting filters, not mapping.

filters.search_placeholder: Search by name, description or slug...

table.name: Name
table.empty: No sub-regions yet - add one to start segmenting your tenant geographically.

form.name: Name
form.slug_description: Lowercase, hyphenated identifier used in URLs and filters.

sub_regions.confirm_mark_inactive: Mark "{{name}}" as inactive? Linked care providers will be detached from it.

meta.title: Success Stories
meta.subtitle: Create and manage proof cards that demonstrate the community's impact - designed for sharing with procurement officers, funders, and municipal partners.

about.title: About this page
about.body: Proof cards are structured impact stories tied to live metrics from the platform. Each card shows a before/after comparison with a cited evidence source and methodology note. Cards marked as "Demo" are illustrative only; mark a card as "Real evidence" once you have verified the data. Published cards appear in the Municipal Impact Report and the public-facing impact page.

empty.title: No success stories yet
empty.body: Seed 3 illustrative demo cards to show what proof cards look like to procurement officers and funders. They are clearly labelled "Demo / illustrative" until you mark them as real evidence by hand.

information_distribution.title: 30% lower information-distribution effort
information_distribution.narrative: Coordinators spend roughly a third less time distributing updates after switching to NEXUS. Announcements, member-segment targeting, and confirmation tracking are consolidated in one place.
information_distribution.method_caveat: Illustrative example based on a peer-community claim; it has not been measured for this tenant.

volunteer_engagement.title: 25% more volunteer engagement
volunteer_engagement.narrative: Pilot communities report a quarter more active volunteer participation after adopting NEXUS, supported by clearer matching, low-friction sign-up, and visible Warmth Pass recognition.
volunteer_engagement.method_caveat: Illustrative example based on a peer-community claim; it has not been measured for this tenant.

formal_care_offset.title: CHF 12,250 in formal-care costs offset
formal_care_offset.narrative: Volunteer hours coordinated through NEXUS represent a CHF 12,250 offset against formal home-care costs during the pilot window, valued at the Swiss formal-care assistant rate.
formal_care_offset.method_caveat: Estimate using CHF 35 per hour across 350 hours; the comparison is against the pre-pilot baseline only.

editor.title: Title
editor.narrative_placeholder: 2-4 sentences explaining the story to a procurement officer or funder.
editor.metric_source_description: pilot_scoreboard: auto-pulls from AG83; municipal_roi: auto-pulls from AG76; manual: you enter values by hand.
editor.method_caveat_placeholder: e.g. "Pilot region only; n=42 members; 90-day window"
editor.method_caveat_description: Required - a one-sentence note on methodology limitations. Keeps claims honest with funders. Example: 'Based on self-reported hours; external audit pending.'
editor.evidence_source_description: Required - where the number comes from. Example: 'NEXUS platform data, 90-day rolling window ending 2026-04-01' or 'Swiss Federal Statistical Office 2023.'
editor.demo_description: Card is labelled as demo on the gallery
